settings.title: Puppy Intro Groom
settings.summary: A short, gentle first visit to get young dogs used to the grooming routine.
settings.description:
The aim here is a calm, positive start rather than a perfect cut. A puppy's first visits shape how they feel about grooming for years to come, so we keep everything gentle, short, and reassuring.

What the first visit looks like

We keep it brief: a light wash, a soft dry, and a little trim around the eyes and paws so they get used to the sounds and handling. We introduce the clippers and dryer slowly, letting your puppy sniff and settle before anything starts.

  • A gentle wash and a soft dry
  • A small tidy around the eyes and paws
  • An easy introduction to the tools and the table

Plenty of praise throughout helps them look forward to coming back, and we go entirely at your puppy's pace. If they have had enough partway through, we stop, since a happy ending matters more than finishing every step on day one.

Who it suits and getting ready

It is for young dogs who are new to grooming, ideally once they have had their vaccinations so they can mix safely with others. Older rescue dogs who are wary of grooming can benefit from the same gentle approach, so just ask.

Before you come, a short walk and a chance to go to the toilet help your puppy arrive relaxed. Touching their paws, ears, and face during play at home is the best preparation you can give them, and it makes every future visit easier.
